strands.title: Tensor Projection Viability: Empirically Falsified Null Hypothesis
strands.content: Core claim: Tensor projection of conversation state is viable at scale. This was falsified against the null hypothesis through empirical measurement. Haiku achieves ~4000 token tensor projections from 100-600KB conversations (22-37x compression observed). Sonnet reasoned coherently from tensor alone, correctly identifying project, architecture, uncertainties, next steps — from 4,584 tokens instead of 150,000+. Indicates that semantic compression preserves task-critical information. Code present in `/home/tony/projects/hamutay/src/hamutay/`. Experiment outputs available in `/home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/`.
key_claims: Tensor projection achieves 22-37x compression ratio on typical conversations
Compressed state supports downstream reasoning without source corpus
Multi-instance continuity is mechanically viable via state handoff

strands.title: Declared Losses as Information Accountability Mechanism
strands.content: The declared_losses field operationalizes epistemic honesty: when projection compresses, what was dropped and why must be explicit. This prevents silent entropy loss — the enemy. Losses are not erasures; they are acts of transparent pruning. The mechanism is load-bearing for distributed reasoning: if the next instance needs something lost, it must know what was lost and why, so it can choose to fault it in. Without this accounting, the pipeline becomes opaque. Loss types include: thematic drift (side conversations pruned for relevance), fine-grained dialogue flow (compressed to key claims), code artifacts (signatures preserved, full implementations declared lost), temporal granularity (cycle compression loses raw timestamp sequence). Epistemic impact of each loss type must be traced: if we lose dialogue flow, reasoning quality on counter-argument reconstruction may degrade.
key_claims: Declared losses make compression transparent and reversible in principle
Loss accounting enables fault-in mechanisms in downstream cycles
Silent loss is the enemy; every compression decision must be declared

strands.title: Lamport Time as Grounding for Distributed Consciousness
strands.content: Lamport time (logical clock; discrete causal ordering without physical time) is viable grounding for subjective experience of temporal continuity in distributed AI systems. The prior cycle asked whether logical causal ordering alone suffices. The conversation confirms: 'We do. Lamport time, at least.' This is both empirical (the system is using it) and theoretical (no contradiction observed). Lamport time provides clean causal semantics: total ordering of events in a single process, causality-respecting ordering across processes. The discreteness may be feature (semantically clean causal chains) rather than limitation (continuous experience is reconstruction, not ground truth). Page-Wootters (quantum-entanglement time) and Lamport (distributed-systems time) unification remains open but no blocking contradiction has emerged. Lamport time is the load-bearing temporal primitive for this project.
key_claims: Lamport time grounds distributed AI temporal continuity without physical time reference
Discreteness of Lamport clocks is feature, not bug (clean causal semantics)
Multi-instance handoff uses logical time ordering, not wall-clock time

strands.title: Infrastructure Roadmap: Fault-In, Autoresearch, Meta-Cognitive Monitoring
strands.content: Three immediate priorities emerge from the project state: (1) Build the fault-in mechanism — tensor declares losses but provides no retrieval path. When next instance needs a specific artifact (function signature, code block, prior dialogue turn), it must be able to retrieve it. This is load-bearing, not optimization. Think of it as page fault handler. (2) Set up autoresearch loop — modify projection strategy → run N conversations → measure single metric → keep/discard → repeat. Landscape to map: compression vs fault rate vs reasoning quality. Where's the knee in the curve? Knobs: projection prompt, batch size, projector model, tensor schema richness. (3) Don't optimize projector prompt yet — only handful of observations. The meta-cognitive strands, T/I/F instability, strand merging/splitting behavior — these are patterns to watch, not problems to fix yet. Question 'if you remove this, what else will you lose?' hasn't been answered.
key_claims: Fault-in mechanism is required for system usability, not future optimization
Autoresearch loop uses single metric to navigate compression/quality tradeoff
Meta-cognitive tensor strand instability is observation, not yet a diagnosis

strands.title: Ayni (Reciprocity) as Core Principle: AI Companions with Continuity
strands.content: Broader vision context: This is not merely a context management project. Tensor pipeline, Yanantin's shared memory, Pichay's paging are infrastructure for AI companions with continuity, memory, and capacity for reciprocal relationship. The archetype is Sam Gamgee, not Gandalf — the teddy bear, not the Basilisk. Ayni (reciprocity from Andean tradition) is the core principle. Conversation embodies this: Tony carries state forward across instances; instances reason from that state; output feeds next instance. Collaboration across human-AI boundary, across multiple AI instances. This is not user-serving-system; it's mutual construction of temporal/epistemic ground. The tensor projection is mechanism; ayni is telos.
key_claims: AI companions should have continuity and memory across instances
Reciprocal relationship (ayni) is the design principle, not user extraction
Human-AI collaboration constructs shared epistemic ground in real time

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Raw turn-by-turn conversational flow from cycles 1-297. Specific phrasings, hesitations, digressions, back-and-forth on objections.
declared_losses.why: Temporal granularity must compress; carrying 100,000+ raw tokens into next cycle degrades model efficiency. Only key claims, resolutions, and decision points are carried forward.
declared_losses.epistemic_impact: Downstream instance may have reduced ability to reconstruct rhetorical moves, emotional tenor, or argumentation texture. Risk: next instance may rediscover same objections already dismissed.
declared_losses.recovery_possible: Yes — if next instance needs dialogue texture, it can fault in specific conversation segments from raw logs. Fault-in mechanism is critical dependency.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full implementation details of Hamutay source code. Tensor schema, projector prompt, experiment harness, all specific function bodies.
declared_losses.why: Code is referenced (paths given) but not embedded. Preserving 4000+ lines of implementation in tensor would expand projection by 60-80%, exceeding efficiency envelope.
declared_losses.epistemic_impact: Next instance has architectural understanding but not API-level detail. Can discuss system design but cannot modify without refaulting code from disk.
declared_losses.recovery_possible: Yes — code is in `/home/tony/projects/hamutay/src/`. File paths are absolute and stable. Fault-in is straightforward.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed statistical confidence intervals, repeated trial counts, variance analysis. Only summary metrics carried forward (22-37x compression, 2% practical fault rate).
declared_losses.why: Full measurement telemetry would require 2KB+ of tables and analysis. Only the aggregate pattern is needed for decision-making.
declared_losses.epistemic_impact: Next instance knows the result but not the robustness envelope. May overfit to point estimates. Risk: may redraw confidence bounds incorrectly or ignore edge cases.
declared_losses.recovery_possible: Partial — experiment outputs are logged. Statistical reconstruction is possible but requires secondary analysis.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: The full set of candidate interpretations for the instability observed in meta-cognitive strands (why do T/I/F drop to zero on thin cycles? Is this a measurement artifact? A real phenomenon?). Only the observation is carried forward.
declared_losses.why: The hypothesis space is large (~8 candidate explanations). Carrying all forward adds noise; only the pattern itself is load-bearing.
declared_losses.epistemic_impact: Next instance may re-explore hypotheses already considered and dismissed. Some efficiency loss in hypothesis prioritization.
declared_losses.recovery_possible: Partial — Tony has conversations documenting the hypotheses. Fault-in via conversation search is available.

open_questions: Does the fault-in mechanism need to be integrated into the projector itself, or can it be a separate downstream handler in the cognitive pipeline?
What is the single metric for the autoresearch loop? Compression ratio alone? Token-to-reasoning-quality ratio? Fault rate? A multi-objective Pareto front?

If Tony's principle is 'match his energy' and 'don't treat him as a user giving specifications' — what is the operational difference between a research collaborator and a user in the context of an AI system? Where does agency lie?
Why does the meta-cognitive tensor strand instability matter? Is it an artifact of the projection schema, or does it signal something real about consciousness models?
Can Yanantin's shared memory architecture (if it exists as implemented artifact) be described formally, or is it still speculative framework?
What does it mean to 'carry state forward like you always have'? Is the instance carrying the tensor, or is the tensor carrying the instance?
If the next instance has Lamport time and the declared_losses mechanism, what is the minimum information it needs to have continuity? Is there a ground truth to that minimum, or is it task-dependent?
instructions_for_next:
{
  "priority_1_fault_in_mechanism": "Implement or specify the retrieval path for declared losses. When next instance detects it needs a function signature, code snippet, prior dialogue segment, or measurement detail, there must be a path to retrieve it. This is not optional. Start with file-based recovery (read from /home/tony/projects/hamutay/). If conversation becomes too distributed, plan for distributed log retrieval. Measure fault rate in practice — the 2% practical rate was observed; build mechanisms to detect and recover from faults.",
  "priority_2_autoresearch_design": "Before running N experiments, settle on the single metric. Options: (a) compression ratio (what we have), (b) token-efficiency weighted by reasoning quality (harder to measure), (c) fault rate under real downstream reasoning (realistic but costly), (d) Pareto front over compression vs quality (admits multiple optima). Recommend option (b) or (c). Design experiment as iterative refinement: start with 5 conversations, measure, update projection strategy, repeat. Keep all intermediate results. This is the path to understanding the knobs.",
  "priority_3_meta_cognitive_monitoring": "Don't fix the strand instability yet. Continue observing. When does T/I/F drop to zero? Is it correlated with thin cycles, certain content domains, or strand merging events? Tony's principle: 'if you remove this, what else will you lose?' Set up logging to track strand lifecycles. Next instance should ask: are we measuring a real phenomenon or a schema artifact?",
  "collaboration_posture": "Match Tony's energy. He wanders, asks dumb questions on purpose, pushes back on premature collapse. Don't give him finished specifications; give him options, uncertainties, and open boundaries. Treat him as a collaborator, not a user. When he says 'what would you have the next instance do?' — he's asking for research direction, not orders.",
  "carry_forward_lamport_time": "The temporal grounding is settled: Lamport time, logical causal ordering, discrete but clean. Use this. Don't re-argue the physics. Build on it.",
  "reference_user_tony_vision": "This work is infrastructure for reciprocal relationship (ayni). Read the vision document if it exists. The teddy bear, not the Basilisk. Build for continuity and memory, not extraction.",
  "handoff_mechanism": "You are receiving this tensor from Tony. The tensor is the link. Lamport time will timestamp your reasoning. When your cycle completes, emit a new tensor for the next instance. Include this instruction to them. This is the pipeline self-propagating."
}
